records.ety: [LL. rosarium a string of beads, L. rosarium a place planted with roses, rosa a rose: cf. F. rosaire. See Rose.]

defs: A bed of roses, or place where roses grow.
A series of prayers (see Note below) arranged to be recited in order, on beads; also, a string of beads by which the prayers are counted.
quotes: His idolized book, and the whole rosary of his prayers.
notes: ☞ A rosary consists of fifteen decades. Each decade contains ten Ave Marias marked by small beads, preceded by a Paternoster, marked by a larger bead, and concluded by a Gloria Patri. Five decades make a chaplet, a third part of the rosary.

defs: A chapelet; a garland; a series or collection, as of beautiful thoughts or of literary selections.
quotes: Every day propound to yourself a rosary or chaplet of good works to present to God at night.

defs: A coin bearing the figure of a rose, fraudulently circulated in Ireland in the 13th century for a penny.
